Off Calendar Apr-08-2024 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOHN VANNUCCI, Esq. of Law Offices of John Fitzpatrick Vannucci (tel: 415-981-7500) for Plaintiff.
Atty. MICHAEL DIKKINGHAM, Esq. (e-mail: mdillingham@gmail.com) for Defendant Robert L. Elam.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ROBERT ELAM's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Robert Elam's demurrer to plaintiff Evan Krow's complaint is ordered off calendar.

Robert Elam, erroneously sued as "Bobby" Elam, is in default and cannot participate in litigation until further steps are taken. (See Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385.) "[A] party regularly served with summons, although under a name not his own, must come in and set up the misnomer and whatever defense he may have, or else be held concluded by the judgment." (Brum v. Ivins (1908) 154 Cal. 17, 20.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court Reporter: Mary Argyropoulos, CSR 775 (tel: 925-310-5515/e-mail: mary@argyreporting.com). (302/RCE)

Off Calendar Jul-14-2023 Continued To Sep-13-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT ROBERT ELAM MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Robert Elam's motion to quash service of summons is denied. CCP 415.20(b) provides that "a summons may be served by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint at the person's usual place of business, or usual mailing address and by thereafter mailing a copy of the summons and of the complaint by first-class mail" to that same location. That is what happened here. Summons was left with the receptionist for Elam's law firm (and others) at 90 New Montgomery Street in San Francisco - the address Elam's firm reports to the state bar and states on its website and, indeed, on the cover of Elam's motion to quash. (Opp. 4:6-5:20.) A copy of the summons was thereafter mailed to that same address. (Id. at 5:8-9.) Claiming an unspecified Nevada residence, Elam argues that the receptionist was not specifically authorized to accept service of summons for him. But CCP 415.20(b)'s plain language includes no such requirement, nor does Elam cite any authority to that effect. Also, Elam concedes receiving mail and deliveries at 90 New Montgomery and that it is a venue for his client meetings. (Memo. 1:14-24.) In short, Elam's office is a CCP 415.20(b) "usual place of business" and "usual mailing address" for Elam.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
